title: Working with Images
subtitle: edit, create, and explore!
overview: This session introduces students to digital images, image editing tools, and creative design concepts. Students will learn how to crop, resize, draw, add text, and apply effects to images using beginner-friendly software tools.

images._note: Add classroom photos, image editing practice screenshots, student creativity work, and digital art examples.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of image editing software
gallery.caption: Students learning basic image editing tools
Crop Tool — remove unwanted parts from an image

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of adding text on image
gallery.caption: Text Tool — write captions and titles

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of drawing using brush tool
gallery.caption: Brush Tool — digital drawing and painting
Filters and Effects make images creative

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of students creating posters
gallery.caption: Students designing creative posters

keyTopics: Pixels — Tiny dots that make a picture
Resize Tool — Make image bigger or smaller
Rotate Tool — Turn image left or right
Text Tool — Add words and captions
Filters and Effects — Improve image appearance
Save vs Save As — Understanding file formats

activity.title: Create a Creative Poster Using Images
steps: Insert a picture into the workspace
Crop unwanted parts from the image
Add text with a title and caption
Save the final poster as JPG or PNG
Present the poster to the class

quiz.q: What is a digital image made of?
quiz.explanation: Digital images are made of tiny dots called pixels.

quiz.q: Which tool is used to remove unwanted parts of an image?
quiz.explanation: Crop Tool helps remove unwanted areas from an image.

quiz.q: What does the Resize Tool do?
quiz.explanation: Resize Tool makes an image bigger or smaller.

quiz.q: Which tool is used to add words to an image?
quiz.explanation: Text Tool is used to add titles and captions.

quiz.q: What does the Rotate Tool do?
quiz.explanation: Rotate Tool turns the image left, right, or upside down.

quiz.q: Which tool is best for digital drawing?
quiz.explanation: Brush Tool allows freehand drawing and painting.

quiz.q: Why are filters used in image editing?
quiz.explanation: Filters make images look creative and attractive.

quiz.q: Which file format is commonly used for images?
quiz.explanation: JPG is one of the most common image file formats.

quiz.q: What happens when image size becomes too large?
quiz.explanation: Large images take more storage and load slowly.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of Save As?
options: Save with a new name or format
quiz.explanation: Save As creates a new copy with a different name or format.

quiz.q: Which software can be used for online image editing?
quiz.explanation: Pixlr is a popular online image editing tool.
Thousands of pixels combine to create a digital image.

quiz.q: What should students do after completing image editing?
quiz.explanation: Always save your creative work properly.

quiz.q: What is creativity in image editing?
quiz.explanation: Creativity means making unique and attractive designs using imagination.

quiz.q: Which activity helps students learn image editing better?
quiz.explanation: Hands-on practice improves digital creativity and editing skills.
